zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Francis於1362年出生在英國米德萊可艾德蒙頓時，她的父親Sir Adam Francis Lord Mayor of London48歲，母親Agnes Champneis24歲。她於1381年11月2日之前與Sir Alan Buxhull在英國米德萊可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1424年8月5日在英國波克夏比善姆享年62歲，埋葬在英國波克夏比善姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud Francis于1362年出生在英国米德萊可艾德蒙頓时，她的父亲Sir Adam Francis Lord Mayor of London48岁，母亲Agnes Champneis24岁。她于1381年11月2日之前与Sir Alan Buxhull在英国米德萊可结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1424年8月5日在英国波克夏比善姆享年62岁，埋葬在英国波克夏比善姆。

nameMeanings.name: Francis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Old French personal name Franceis, modern French François, from Latin Franciscus, originally ‘Frank’, though later used to denote a Frenchman. The personal name owed much of its popularity during the Middle Ages to the fame of Saint Francis of Assisi (1181–1226), whose baptismal name was actually Giovanni but who was nicknamed Francisco because his father was absent in France at the time of his birth. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Francesco and Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian, and Croatian Franc , and also their derivatives, e.g. Croatian Francišković (see Francisco 4).
English: ethnic name from the Old French adjective Franceis ‘French’ (see 1 above).
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames, or an adoption of the non-Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ferenc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Casimir, Iwan, Zbigniew.
Hungarian, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia and Poland), Slovenian, and Croatian: from the personal name Ferenc, a Hungarian form of Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Fraczek
meanings: Polish (Frączek): from a pet form of the personal name Franciszek, an equivalent of Latin Franciscus (see Francis ). It is found mainly in Lesser Poland.

variants.name: Chico
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jesus, Pedro, Rafael, Bernardo, Camilo, Cirilo, Juan, Miguel, Ramona, Ruben.
Spanish: from chico ‘small, young’ (of uncertain origin, perhaps from Latin ciccum ‘trifle’), applied as a nickname for a small man, or for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name.
Portuguese: from a pet form of the personal name Francisco, a vernacular form of Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Francesco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Gabriele, Giovanni, Pasquale, Rocco, Salvatore.  Spanish Carlota, Garcia, Romana.
Italian: from the personal name Francesco (from Latin Franciscus), an equivalent of Francis .

variants.name: Di Cicco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Pasquale, Carlo, Cosmo, Giovanni, Giulio, Giuseppe, Nicola, Attilio, Benedetto, Carmela.
Italian: patronymic from a pet form of the personal name Francesco, from Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Ceccarelli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Dino, Angelo, Dante, Quinto, Sesto, Aldo, Americo, Amerigo, Arcangelo, Augusto, Aurelio, Franco, Gino, Guido, Italo, Leoncio, Renato, Roberto, Tino, Tito, Valerio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Ceccarello, a diminutive of Cecco, itself a diminutive Francesco, from Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Di Cioccio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Cosmo, Mario, Cesidio, Dante, Guido, Salvatore, Aldo, Americo, Benito, Biagio, Domenico, Ezio, Flavio, Nunzio, Quintino, Silverio.
Italian: patronymic from a personal name of uncertain derivation,possibly a pet form of Francesco (see Francis ).

variants.name: Cillo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Americo, Aniello, Antonio, Carmine, Gianna, Giovanni, Guido, Livio, Rocco, Vito.
Italian: from a shortened pet form of Francesco (equivalent of Francis ).

variants.name: Cisco
meanings: Italian (Veneto): from the personal name Cisco, a short form of Francesco, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).
American shortened form of the surname   Francisco   (which is in part of German and presumably also of French origin) and thus a variant of   Sisco   and   Sesco  .

variants.name: Fran
meanings: Croatian: from a short form of local equivalents of the Latin personal name Franciscus (see Francis ).
Spanish: probably an altered form of Catalan   Franc  .
Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from a short form of the Spanish personal name   Franco   or   Francisco  .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Francis was born in 1362, in Edmonton, Middlesex, England, her father, Sir Adam Francis Lord Mayor of London, was 48 and her mother, Agnes Champneis, was 24. She married Sir Alan Buxhull before 2 November 1381, in Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 5 August 1424, in Bisham, Berkshire, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in Bisham, Berkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Francis est née en 1362, à Edmonton, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Adam Francis Lord Mayor of London, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Agnes Champneis, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Alan Buxhull avant 2 novembre 1381, à Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 5 août 1424, à Bisham, Berkshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Bisham, Berkshire, Angleterre.

de.St. Helen Bishopsgate, London, England: St Helen Bishopsgate, London, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Francis 1362 in Edmonton, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Adam Francis Lord Mayor of London 48 und ihre Mutter Agnes Champneis 24. Sie hat vor 2. November 1381 in Middlesex, England Sir Alan Buxhull geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 5. August 1424 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Bisham, Berkshire, England und wurde in Bisham, Berkshire, England begraben.

Lei sposato Sir Alan Buxhull prima del 2 novembre 1381, nel Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 5 agosto 1424, nel Bisham, Berkshire, England, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a Bisham, Berkshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Francis(이)가 1362년 영국 미들레세스 에드몽에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Adam Francis Lord Mayor of London(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Champneis(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1381년 11월 2일 이전 영국 미들레세스에서 Sir Alan Buxhull(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1424년 8월 5일 영국 버크실르 비샤에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 버크실르 비샤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Francis nasceu em 1362, em Edmonton, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Adam Francis Lord Mayor of London, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Champneis, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Alan Buxhull antes 2 de novembro de 1381, em Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 5 de agosto de 1424, em Bisham, Berkshire, Inglaterra, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Bisham, Berkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.St. Helen Bishopsgate, London, England: ст хелен бишопсгэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Francis родилась в 1362 г. в местности эдмонтон, миддлесекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Adam Francis Lord Mayor of London было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Champneis, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Alan Buxhull перед 2 ноябрь 1381 г. в местности миддлесекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 5 августа 1424 г. в местности бишам, биркшир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности бишам, биркшир, Англия.
